examples: Harbor load-in: replaced checklist-style packing summary with hands-on dock labor so the beat stays lived instead of procedural.
Sea-opposition onset: revised the bread-taste flare from symptom notation into a carry-forward sensory hit tied to Stratholme residue.
Rail exchange: cut the 'That should have been bait' explanation so Sylvanas's line lands through tone and visible behavior.
observations: The opening descent into Seastone Harbor stays in Jaina's lane through cold, horses, and the ship read before any theory intrudes.
Dagny's first appearance sounds like a working captain, not a colorful port NPC; the chapter keeps her blunt and practical.
The departure pages hold the endgame register: lean, salt-cold, and stripped of the earlier-book wit surplus.
Jaina's narration stays built rather than florid; even her longer magic thoughts remain tied to immediate ship function.
The dawn and landfall pages keep the prose hard and local enough for chapter20's late-book pressure.

notes: POV stayed clean. The chapter never leaves Jaina's body, ship-sense, or inference lane.
observations: Dagny is read through boots, voice, sail handling, and bargaining pressure rather than narrator summary.
Sylvanas's care remains external-only: waterskin, ladder rung, oilskin, station-keeping, command tone.
Cyndia stays legible through dry remarks and task competence without any access to interior motive.
Northrend pressure arrives through taste, cold, deck motion, and resonance response rather than omniscient framing.
Arthas's lead is inferred from dock cuts, frozen ruts, and the inland pull; the prose does not claim knowledge Jaina cannot have.

examples: Mid-voyage broth beat: restored the bread-taste as an already-established baseline instead of letting the symptom feel newly introduced.
notes: The Antonidas stone remains dead weight, not a reopened conversation lane.
The chapter remembers chapter19's institutional severance and never restages the choice north as undecided.
observations: Chapter19's salt-edge road residue carries directly into the harbor descent and keeps the handoff clean.
Jaina and Sylvanas continue operating as a chosen pair rather than resetting to distance or fresh caution.
The broadcaster-source thread evolves correctly: north sharpens, the sea fight answers back, and the pull widens near landfall.
Northrend arrives as already-chosen destination and hostile condition, not as fresh worldbuilding reveal.
The closing evidence at Daggercap Bay keeps Arthas ahead without overexplaining what chapter21 will read on foot.

examples: Harbor gear paragraph: broke the checklist cadence into a more elastic action sentence with human hands in it.
Certainty exchange: replaced an explanatory pause sentence with a shorter physical beat so the dialogue turns cleaner.
observations: The harbor opening alternates blunt visual anchors with longer descent sentences, which suits the chapter's threshold work.
The storm-work section uses clipped command fragments effectively; the repetitions read as ship rhythm, not accidental monotony.
Dagny's deck orders provide needed short-beat percussion inside the heavier sea-action paragraphs.
The below-deck passage slows just enough to reset the ear before the Northrend approach.
The final bay read now lands on shorter, colder beats before the inland pull resumes.

examples: Ladder-rung stop: tightened Sylvanas's correction into concrete command and bodily limit, matching the chapter test about Jaina accepting an external stop.
Landfall deduction: replaced thesis-style timing summary with frozen-rut evidence and widening inland pull so the handoff into chapter21 stays causal.
continuity.crossChapter: Chapter19's coast-road salt, dead stone, and narrowed support structure carry straight into Seastone Harbor and then into Daggercap Bay.
observations: Harbor negotiation leads naturally into boarding, horse handoff, and departure without any temporal throat-clearing.
The sea-opposition beat grows out of the sharpened north pull rather than appearing as unrelated supernatural weather.
Sylvanas stopping Jaina on deck pays off prior command-as-care language without softening the relationship out of character.
The chapter preserves the plan's causal chain from sea resistance to bodily cost to trust-lane to arrival evidence.
The close leaves chapter21 exactly where it should begin: shore underfoot, harbor traces visible, inland drag active.

examples: Removed the authorial bait-label in the rail exchange so the prose stops commenting on its own sparring rhythm.
Removed the explicit 'Weeks, Jaina thought' / restatement pair in favor of physical harbor evidence and embodied lateness.
observations: The chapter opens on active descent and ship-read, not on weather throat-clearing or recap.
No biography clauses were reintroduced for Dagny, the crew, or the horses once the chapter was moving.
The strongest surviving slop risk was explanatory stage-direction around emotional beats; the key instances were trimmed.
The arrival sequence now trusts dock cuts, char, silence, and frozen ruts to do the heavy lifting.
Scene endings generally stop on movement, order, or pressure rather than on abstract moral summary.

examples: Sylvanas: 'remain someone who can stand' -> 'stay on your feet' to keep the order in spoken command language.
Sylvanas: 'mistaking damage for usefulness' -> 'trying to pay for everything in blood' to keep the line concrete and character-native.
notes: No hitlist offenders such as vector, actionable, optimal, or ingested survived in spoken lines.
observations: Dagny's speech stays practical, maritime, and unromantic even while pricing danger and reading weather.
Jaina speaks plainly aboard ship and keeps her technical lane compressed to what the moment can bear.
Sylvanas stays clipped and authoritative without falling into generic one-word stoicism.
Cyndia's few lines remain dry field humor, not bureaucratic cleverness.
The chapter's quoted speech sounds spoken aloud on a wet deck, not written into a briefing.
